question_text: Which of the following is an example of refraction?

options.label: B
options.text: A straw appearing bent when placed in a glass of water

options.label: C
options.text: A shadow forming behind an object

options.label: D
options.text: Light travelling in a straight line through air

correct_answer: A straw appearing bent when placed in a glass of water
explanation: Refraction is the bending of light as it passes from one material to another. When light travels from water into air (or vice versa), it changes speed and direction. This makes the straw appear bent at the water's surface, even though it is straight. The other options show reflection (bouncing off a mirror), shadow formation, and light travelling straight through a uniform material.
